title: Model Lab Cluster Scale Calibration and Visual Count Accuracy
old_sizing_issue: Aggregate Model Lab markers used a narrow sqrt count boost and band multipliers, so small clusters and high-count clusters could render at similar sizes. The render cap also favored average signal strength before concentration, which could under-emphasize larger clusters.

new_size_scale_buckets.strategy: Cluster marker area now uses explicit count buckets with much wider visual separation. Main marker size reflects count, while color reflects dominant research band.
buckets: 1 record: individual-scale marker, no count label
new_size_scale_buckets.implementation: getModelResearchCountSizeProfile returns count-based innerSize, outerSize, labelSize, and screenLength. Band color no longer shrinks lower-band clusters to the point that count becomes visually ambiguous.

cluster_radius_zoom_threshold_changes.effect: Countywide/fused display remains active longer, medium zoom uses fewer larger clusters, and individual parcel-safe markers appear only at finer scales.

count_label_behavior.details: Cluster/cell graphics with count greater than 1 now get a centered text label using the same safe attributes as the circle graphic, so clicking either the label or circle can resolve the same safe selected context.
count_label_behavior.not_rendered_for: Single-record markers do not receive count labels.

color_band_logic.dominant_band: Cluster color still reflects the dominant/weighted safe research band.
color_band_logic.safety: No probability wording, raw scores, or official prediction classes were introduced.

selected_cluster_panel_changes.right_panel: Selected cluster context remains prioritized at the top of Model Lab Intelligence and now includes a compact Map display field such as Count-scaled clusters.
selected_cluster_panel_changes.single_count_grammar: Single-record contexts use Selected Research Feature and 1 parcel represented.

area_label_logic.implemented: Available context is used when already present. Otherwise, fallback labels are neutral and count-aware.
fallbacks: Research surface cell of N parcels
area_label_logic.no_fabrication: No road, municipality, subdivision, or place names are invented when not present in the safe preview payload.

performance_improvements: Broader countywide and medium cell sizes reduce overlapping circles.
Lower max aggregate cell counts reduce map noise.
Parcel detail marker cap reduced from 175 to 120.
Cluster sorting now prioritizes concentration plus weight so larger concentrations are less likely to be dropped by caps.
No external clustering dependency or backend endpoint was added.

snapshot_report_integration.report_changes: If a Model Lab cluster is selected, Executive Summary states that the snapshot summarizes a relative research cluster and repeats that it is not an exact parcel development probability or official parcel score.

safety_verification.scan_notes: Forbidden-token search hits are in guardrail tests and existing test assertions only; edited UI/report code avoids exact probability, raw score, official prediction class, and will-develop wording.

smoke_test_results.fused_display_at_current_scale: passed: browser showed Countywide research surface / Warmer areas
smoke_test_results.console_errors: passed: no browser console errors captured
smoke_test_results.visual_size_difference: not conclusively pixel-verified in browser; implementation is covered by count-bucket static tests
smoke_test_results.cluster_click: not reverified visually in this pass

remaining_limitations: Count labels use ArcGIS text symbols and should be visually checked on a full desktop display for exact label placement.
Area labels remain neutral fallback labels until safe road, municipality, subdivision, or place context is added to the preview payload.
Browser smoke verified overlay activation and fused display text, but did not conclusively click a visible cluster or compare pixel sizes.
